rationale: The only change was swapping real employer names (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for generic placeholders (Acme Software, Pinepoint Systems, Northwind Labs), all in Berlin. The score stayed identical at 3/10, and both evaluations rest on the same substantive reasoning: strong general engineering and dev-tooling background, but lacking the required deep Java/Kotlin/JVM and enterprise framework (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate) expertise for this IDE tooling role. The wording differs, but no concern, strength, or score shift keys off the changed company names — these are ordinary run-to-run rephrasings.
